description: Deploy Grafana Tempo and Grafana Alloy to provide OTLP trace collection on local (k3d) and dev (Linode k3s) environments. Services are already instrumented with OpenTelemetry via tdp-fastapi-core, but no trace backend exists — this adds the missing pipeline: services → Alloy (OTLP receiver) → Tempo (storage) → Grafana (query/visualisation).

userStories.title: Deploy Grafana Tempo via Pulumi
userStories.description: As a platform engineer, I want Grafana Tempo deployed via Pulumi Helm chart so that there is a trace storage backend available in all environments. Create infra/platform/charts/tempo.ts with monolithic mode, environment-aware retention and storage config. Add to infra/platform/index.ts deployment sequence (after monitoring).
acceptanceCriteria: infra/platform/charts/tempo.ts exists with Helm chart deployment for grafana/tempo
Tempo is deployed in monolithic mode in the monitoring namespace
Retention and storage are environment-aware (ephemeral for local, local-path PV for dev)
tempo.ts is wired into infra/platform/index.ts deployment sequence after monitoring
Resource tiers follow existing minimal/standard pattern from monitoring.ts
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Effort: M. Follow existing Helm chart patterns in infra/platform/charts/.

userStories.title: Deploy Grafana Alloy via Pulumi
userStories.description: As a platform engineer, I want Grafana Alloy deployed via Pulumi Helm chart so that services have an OTLP receiver to send traces to. Create infra/platform/charts/alloy.ts with DaemonSet mode, OTLP gRPC receiver on port 4317, export to Tempo. Expose via ClusterIP Service for in-cluster access.
acceptanceCriteria: infra/platform/charts/alloy.ts exists with Helm chart deployment for grafana/alloy
Alloy is deployed as DaemonSet in the monitoring namespace
OTLP gRPC receiver is configured on port 4317
Alloy exports traces to Tempo
ClusterIP Service exposes Alloy at alloy-otlp.monitoring.svc.cluster.local:4317
alloy.ts is wired into infra/platform/index.ts deployment sequence
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Effort: M. Alloy config uses River syntax for receive → batch → export pipeline.

userStories.title: Wire Tempo datasource into Grafana
userStories.description: As a platform engineer, I want Tempo configured as a Grafana datasource so that traces can be queried and visualised. Add Tempo as an additional datasource in the kube-prometheus-stack Helm values (monitoring.ts). Configure trace-to-metrics correlation.
acceptanceCriteria: Tempo datasource is added to kube-prometheus-stack Helm values in monitoring.ts
Trace-to-metrics correlation is configured linking traces to Prometheus metrics
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Effort: S. Depends on Tempo being deployed (US-001).

userStories.title: Configure OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT in services
userStories.description: As a platform engineer, I want the OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT environment variable set in service Helm values so that services send traces to the Alloy collector. Add the env var to schema-registry values.yaml, values-dev.yaml, and values-local.yaml pointing at the Alloy ClusterIP service. Update the Copier service template to include this by default.
acceptanceCriteria: OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT is set in schema-registry values.yaml, values-dev.yaml, and values-local.yaml
Endpoint points to http://alloy-otlp.monitoring.svc.cluster.local:4317
Copier service template includes OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT by default for new services
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Effort: S. Depends on Alloy being deployed (US-002).

userStories.title: Add NetworkPolicy for service to Alloy egress
userStories.description: As a platform engineer, I want a NetworkPolicy allowing service pods to reach Alloy on port 4317 in the monitoring namespace, so that traces are not blocked by default-deny policies on dev. Add egress policy for pods with app.kubernetes.io/part-of: the-data-platform label.
acceptanceCriteria: NetworkPolicy allows pods with app.kubernetes.io/part-of: the-data-platform to reach Alloy on port 4317 in monitoring namespace
Policy follows same pattern as existing allow-app-to-postgres policy
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Effort: S. Required on dev where default-deny is enforced.

userStories.title: Add Pulumi config keys for tracing
userStories.description: As a platform engineer, I want Pulumi config keys for tempoRetention and tempoStorageSize so that trace retention and storage are configurable per environment. Local: 6h retention, no PV. Dev: 48h retention, 5Gi PV. Production: 7d retention, 20Gi PV.
acceptanceCriteria: tempoRetention config key is added to Pulumi stack configs with per-environment values
tempoStorageSize config key is added to Pulumi stack configs with per-environment values
Local config: 6h retention, no PV
Dev config: 48h retention, 5Gi PV
Production config: 7d retention, 20Gi PV
tempo.ts reads and applies these config values
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Effort: S. Last task in Ralph workstream — human review checkpoint.
